Paragraph

A bounded real-valued function \(f\) defined on an interval \([a, b]\) is called Riemann-Stieltjes integrable with respect to \(d\alpha\text{,}\) if
\begin{equation*} \lowint_a^b\,f\, d\alpha = \upint_a^b\,f\, d\alpha. \end{equation*}
In such a case we write \(f\in \cR(\alpha)\) on \([a, b]\text{,}\) and use \(\int_{a}^{b} \,f\, d\alpha\) for \(\lowint_a^b\,f\, d\alpha \text{.}\)
in-context
